title: Xtra Hearty Syrian White Bean Stew
description: A light and flavorful Syrian dish featuring dried white beans and a robata-grill twist, perfect for a flexitarian-friendly meal. This stew is fermented to create a unique glassy-jelly texture.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a fondue pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the soaked and drained white beans, robata-grill roasted red peppers, tomato paste, vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper to the fondue pot.
Stir well to combine and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes or until the beans are tender.
Use an immersion blender to puree the stew to a glassy-jelly texture, or allow it to cool and refrigerate overnight to allow the flavors to meld and the texture to set.
Reheat the stew over low heat, stirring occasionally, until warmed through.
Serve the Xtra Hearty Syrian White Bean Stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
